source_intake_record.raw_question_text:
As shown in the figure, there is a smooth, insulated long plank $B$ with a vertical barrier attached to its right end, resting stationary on a smooth horizontal ground. A slider $A$, which can be considered as a point mass, is placed on the long plank. The distance between $A$ and the right barrier of $B$ is $L$, and the distance between $A$ and the left end of $B$ is sufficiently large. Both $A$ and $B$ (including the barrier) have a mass of $m$. The slider $A$ carries a charge of $+q$. Initially, both $A$ and $B$ are at rest. If a uniform electric field of magnitude $E$ and direction horizontally to the right is applied in this space, $A$ will start moving to the right due to the electric force and will collide elastically with $B$. The collision between $A$ and $B$ occurs over an extremely short time interval, and there is no charge transfer during the collision. Find:

1. After the first collision between $A$ and $B$, what is the speed of $B$?
2. What is the time elapsed from the start of motion at $A$ until the second collision between $A$ and $B$?
3. What is the distance traveled by the long plank $B$ from the $n$-th collision to the $(n+1)$-th collision between $A$ and $B$?

issues: metadata.image_paths is inconsistent with top-level image_paths: metadata lists images/26ae3e00d6777fae09ba012d38040d8072b773ca967836daa4afe509a9d81c27.jpg while top-level image_paths lists outputs/physreason_full_000_1200/run_8615a51c0fef0d6a/datasets/physreason/artifacts/images/prob_33771d76046f566fdd446acb_primary.png.

image_dependency: question_text explicitly says 'As shown in the figure'
the setup uses spatial relations in the diagram (slider on plank, right barrier, left end, distance L), although much of the geometry is also described in text
multi_step: the problem asks for three linked results: post-collision speed, time to second collision, and a general nth-to-(n+1)th distance formula
solving requires combining electric-force acceleration, elastic collision relations, and repeated-collision motion analysis
answer_text contains symbolic expressions including a general term 4nL, indicating nontrivial sequential reasoning rather than single-step lookup
verifiability: the expected outputs are closed-form symbolic expressions, which are in principle checkable by physics derivation or symbolic equivalence
registry_passed=true, so the core assets appear available for downstream verification

2. What is the time elapsed from the start of motion at $A$ until the second collision between $A$ and $B$?
3. What is the distance traveled by the long plank $B$ from the $n$-th collision to the $(n+1)$-th collision between $A$ and $B$?

field_audit_records.before_value:
As shown in the figure, there is a smooth, insulated long plank $B$ with a vertical barrier attached to its right end, resting stationary on a smooth horizontal ground. A slider $A$, which can be considered as a point mass, is placed on the long plank. The distance between $A$ and the right barrier of $B$ is $L$, and the distance between $A$ and the left end of $B$ is sufficiently large. Both $A$ and $B$ (including the barrier) have a mass of $m$. The slider $A$ carries a charge of $+q$. Initially, both $A$ and $B$ are at rest. If a uniform electric field of magnitude $E$ and direction horizontally to the right is applied in this space, $A$ will start moving to the right due to the electric force and will collide elastically with $B$. The collision between $A$ and $B$ occurs over an extremely short time interval, and there is no charge transfer during the collision. Find:

1. After the first collision between $A$ and $B$, what is the speed of $B$?
2. What is the time elapsed from the start of motion at $A$ until the second collision between $A$ and $B$?
3. What is the distance traveled by the long plank $B$ from the $n$-th collision to the $(n+1)$-th collision between $A$ and $B$?
field_audit_records.after_value:

If a uniform electric field of magnitude $E$ and direction horizontally to the right is applied in this space, $A$ will start moving to the right due to the electric force and will collide elastically with $B$. The collision between $A$ and $B$ occurs over an extremely short time interval, and there is no charge transfer during the collision. Find:

2. What is the time elapsed from the start of motion at $A$ until the second collision between $A$ and $B$?
3. What is the distance traveled by the long plank $B$ from the $n$-th collision to the $(n+1)$-th collision between $A$ and $B$?
